title: Dryopteris nigropaleacea
taxonomy: Dryopteris nigropaleacea is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This species is a part of the genus Dryopteris, which comprises about 250 species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. Dryopteris nigropaleacea is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Southeast Asia, where it grows in humid environments. The classification of D. nigropaleacea reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns in the same family and genus. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of this species.

intro: Dryopteris nigropaleacea, also known as the Black-stalked Wood Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as in protecting against oxidative stress and microbial infections. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris nigropaleacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the most common preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including pain, fever, and respiratory issues.
